steps.title: Read the with phrase
steps.content: The blank appears in a phrase that begins with with: with the even older Hereford map from the fourteenth century. That phrase adds extra information to the sentence rather than forming a new independent clause, so it needs a non-finite verb form after the noun phrase.

steps.title: Avoid a finite verb
steps.content: Choice B uses is, Choice C uses has been, and Choice D uses was. Those are finite verbs, so each would incorrectly turn the phrase after with into a separate clause. The participle being fits the structure: with the even older Hereford map being another one. The answer is A.
